title: lichen_l2_send returns negative errno but net_pkt ownership unclear on partial failure
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:266-354 (lichen_l2_send)

Bug: The function comment at lines 262-265 states ownership semantics: on success (return 0) this function takes ownership and calls net_pkt_unref(); on error (return < 0) caller retains ownership.

However, examining the code:
- Line 293 linearizes packet into tx_ipv6_buf (reads pkt)
- Line 322 calls lichen_link_tx() (uses tx_ipv6_buf, does not touch pkt)
- Line 334 calls lichen_lora_l2_tx() (sends frame, does not touch pkt)
- Line 352 calls net_pkt_unref(pkt) only on success

The concern: if lichen_lora_l2_tx() at line 334 partially succeeds (e.g., sends data to radio but radio driver times out), what happens?

Actually, examining the code more carefully:
1. All error returns before line 352 do NOT call net_pkt_unref()
2. Only line 352 unrefs, which is after success return from lichen_lora_l2_tx()

This matches the documented contract: caller retains ownership on any error. The lora_l2_tx function either fully succeeds or returns error - there is no partial success that would leave unclear ownership.

Closing as not a bug - the ownership semantics are correct.
close_reason: Not a bug - ownership semantics are correct: all error paths return without calling net_pkt_unref(), only success path at line 352 unrefs
